summary: Observed commands: npm run build --prefix mcp 2>&1 | tail -1 && node mcp/dist/cli.js repair --project . 2>&1 | tail -15, node mcp/dist/cli.js learn --project . \ --title "v2.2.0 closed the claude-mem parity gap — full feature map and what remains" \ --learning "Released 2026-06-12 (npm + MCP registry + vendored). Parity achieved vs claude-mem/CMEM in one day across PRs #69-#85: one-shot install, plugin w/ hooks+commands, auto-capture fallback (signal-gated >=0.4 via observationSignalScore), session continuity (kage resume + timeline index), kage repair + remediation errors, <private> tags, SSE live viewer, per-packet discovery_tokens receipts, PreToolUse(Read) injection, personal memory (~/.kage/memory) + kage sync over user git remote, merge-packet git driver + quiet branch refresh, audit-claude-mem wedge, 8 README translations, site pricing tiers. Combined suite 238/238. REMAINING vs them: hosted MCP endpoint (gated on waitlist issue #74 signal), their 80k-star distribution. Strategy: superiority on trust (verify at write/recall/diff), parity on convenience, wedge into their base via audit-claude-mem." \ --paths "CHANGELOG.md" "README.md" "docs/CLOUD.md" \ --type decision --tags release parity claude-mem v2.2.0 2>&1 | tail -1, node mcp/dist/cli.js learn --project . \ --title "CI: kage pr check blocks only on hard-stale memory (still true in v2.2.0)" \ --learning "test learn to see error output" \ --paths "mcp/kernel.ts" --type bug_fix --tags ci 2>&1 | /usr/bin/head -5
body:
Reusable command observation distilled from session 88cc2fa7-9e27-4ee5-948a-0c42baa5bde9: - npm run build --prefix mcp 2>&1 | tail -1 && node mcp/dist/cli.js repair --project . 2>&1 | tail -15: {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "Kage repair — . Memory ✓ packets/workflow-change-memory-release-v2-0-1-d6e19752.json — merge conflict auto-resolved, kept newest side — original saved to .agent_memory/backup/workflow-change-memory-relea {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "Kage repair — . Memory ✓ packets/workflow-change-memory-release-v2-0-1-d6e19752.json — merge conflict auto-resolved, kept newest side — original saved to .agent_memory/backup/workflow-change-memory-release-v2-0-1-d6e19752.json.broken Memory ✓ packets/workflow-stale-catch-feature-landed-via-pr-64-kernel-test-ts-seam-carries-both-v2-test-su-9ad40729.json — merge conflict auto-resolved, kept newest side — original saved to .agent_memory/backup/workflow-stale-catch-feature-landed-via-pr-64-kernel-test-ts-seam-carries-both-v2-test-su-9ad40729.json.broken Locks • lock/temp files — no stale lock or temp files Indexes ✓ indexes + graphs — rebuilt 8 indexes (catalog was out of date) Agents • agent wiring — configured agents look intact 3 fixed, 2 already healthy, 0 failed Validation: passed"} - node mcp/dist/cli.js learn --project . \ --title "v2.2.0 closed the claude-mem parity gap — full feature map and what remains" \ --learning "Released 2026-06-12 (npm + MCP registry + vendored). Parity achieved vs claude-mem/CMEM in one day across PRs #69-#85: one-shot install, plugin w/ hooks+commands, auto-capture fallback (signal-gated >=0.4 via observationSignalScore), session continuity (kage resume + timeline index), kage repair + remediation errors, tags, SSE live viewer, per-packet discovery_tokens receipts, PreToolUse(Read) injection, personal memory (~/.kage/memory) + kage sync over user git remote, merge-packet git driver + quiet branch refresh, audit-claude-mem wedge, 8 README translations, site pricing tiers. Combined suite 238/238. REMAINING vs them: hosted MCP endpoint (gated on waitlist issue #74 signal), their 80k-star distribution. Strategy: superiority on trust (verify at write/recall/diff), parity on convenience, wedge into their base via audit-claude-mem." \ --paths "CHANGELOG.md" "README.md" "docs/CLOUD.md" \ --type decision --tags release parity claude-mem v2.2.0 2>&1 | tail -1: {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "Repo-local memory is written immediately. Promotion to org/global still requires explicit review."} {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "Repo-local memory is written immediately. Promotion to org/global still requires explicit review."} - node mcp/dist/cli.js learn --project . \ --title "CI: kage pr check blocks only on hard-stale memory (still true in v2.2.0)" \ --learning "test learn to see error output" \ --paths "mcp/kernel.ts" --type bug_fix --tags ci 2>&1 | /usr/bin/head -5: {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "Captured session learning: .agent_memory/packets/bug_fix-ci-kage-pr-check-blocks-only-on-hard-stale-memory-still-true-in-v2-2-0-4d874b09.json Repo-local memory is written immediately. Promotion to org/global still requires exp {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "Captured session learning: .agent_memory/packets/bug_fix-ci-kage-pr-check-blocks-only-on-hard-stale-memory-still-true-in-v2-2-0-4d874b09.json Repo-local memory is written immediately. Promotion to org/global still requires explicit review."} - node mcp/dist/cli.js reconcile --project . --json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c " import json,sys d = json.load(sys.stdin) for item in d.get('items', d.get('unresolved', []))[:5]: print(item.get('packet_id') or item.get('id'), '|', (item.get('title') or '')[:70])": {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "repo:https-github-com-kage-core-kage:runbook:releasing-kage-release-js-publishes-from-any-named-branch-1781191414917 | Releasing Kage: release.js publishes from any named branch repo:memory:code_explanation:stale-catch-change- {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "repo:https-github-com-kage-core-kage:runbook:releasing-kage-release-js-publishes-from-any-named-branch-1781191414917 | Releasing Kage: release.js publishes from any named branch repo:memory:code_explanation:stale-catch-change-time-invalidation-heartbeat-stalecatch-kage-staleguard-178118 | Stale-catch: change-time invalidation heartbeat (staleCatch, kage stal repo:agent-a2d7fc6b76e05334a:decision:value-ledger-records-recall-stale-withheld-and-caller-answered-receipts-17811877 | Value ledger records recall, stale-withheld, and caller-answered recei"} - node mcp/dist/cli.js learn --project . \ --title "Releasing Kage: release.js flow current as of v2.2.0" \ --learning "node dist/release.js --publish --push --smoke from mcp/ publishes from any named branch (incl. master): preflights clean worktree, fetches+pushes the branch (rebase first if the kage-sync bot raced you — it pushes [skip ci] commits to master frequently), npm publish with public access, verifies version, smoke-installs the published package. Then: mcp-publisher login http -domain kage-core.com -private-key \$(tr -d '[:space:]' < /tmp/mcp-pub/priv.hex) and mcp-publisher publish (server.json must have BOTH version fields bumped; JWTs expire in hours). Finally upgrade the vendored copy: cp -R mcp/dist/* ~/.claude/kage-mcp/dist/ + package.json (launcher pins it via --mcp-config)." \ --paths "mcp/release.ts" "mcp/server.json" --type runbook --tags release publish 2>/dev/null | /usr/bin/head -1 node mcp/dist/cli.js learn --project . \ --title "Stale-catch: change-time invalidation heartbeat (current through v2.2.0)" \ --learning "staleCatch (kernel.ts) detects memories invalidated by working-tree/diff changes; surfaced by kage pr check lead line and kage staleguard for pre-commit; stale_caught events feed the value ledger and kage gains. Still accurate in v2.2.0 with two additions: quiet refresh on non-default branches computes staleness in memory without persisting metadata rewrites (findings still reported), and personal packets are excluded from stale-catch entirely." \ --paths "mcp/kernel.ts" "mcp/cli.ts" --type code_explanation --tags stale-catch staleguard node mcp/dist/cli.js learn --project . \ --title "Value ledger: recall, stale-withheld, caller-answered + replay receipts (v2.2.0)" \ --learning "Ledger at .agent_memory/reports/value.json records recall_served, stale_withheld, caller_answered, stale_caught; valueSummary windows feed kage gains and the viewer Gains tab; \$ at ~15/1M tokens. v2.2.0 additions: recall_served events carry replay_tokens (sum of served packets' discovery_tokens minus context cost) and receipts report max(read-vs-source, replay); kage gains prints a Knowledge replay value line; non-empty kage file-context injections also record events." \ --paths "mcp/kernel.ts" "mcp/cli.ts" --type decision --tags value-ledger receipts: {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "Captured session learning: .agent_memory/packets/runbook-releasing-kage-release-js-flow-current-as-of-v2-2-0-e798d129.json Captured session learning: .agent_memory/packets/code_explanation-stale-catch-change-time-invalidation- {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "Captured session learning: .agent_memory/packets/runbook-releasing-kage-release-js-flow-current-as-of-v2-2-0-e798d129.json Captured session learning: .agent_memory/packets/code_explanation-stale-catch-change-time-invalidation-heartbeat-current-through-v2-2-0-29ffe1bc.json Repo-local memory is written immediately. Promotion to org/global still requires explicit review. Captured session learning: .agent_memory/packets/decision-value-ledger-recall-stale-withheld-caller-answered-replay-receipts-v2-2-0-b5e59596.json Repo-local memory is written immediately. Promotion to org/global still requires explicit review."} - node mcp/dist/cli.js reconcile --project . --json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c " import json,sys d = json.load(sys.stdin) for item in d.get('items', d.get('unresolved', [])): print(repr(item.get('packet_id') or item.get('id')))": {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "'repo:memory:code_explanation:stale-catch-change-time-invalidation-heartbeat-stalecatch-kage-staleguard-178118' 'repo:agent-a2d7fc6b76e05334a:decision:value-ledger-records-recall-stale-withheld-and-caller-answered-receipts-178 {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "'repo:memory:code_explanation:stale-catch-change-time-invalidation-heartbeat-stalecatch-kage-staleguard-178118' 'repo:agent-a2d7fc6b76e05334a:decision:value-ledger-records-recall-stale-withheld-and-caller-answered-receipts-17811877'"} - node mcp/dist/cli.js learn --project . \ --title "kage sync CI fix: rebase identity fallback + self-healing non-fast-forward push retry" \ --learning "The two kage sync tests failed only on CI (passed on macOS even with bare HOME). Annotated asserts revealed the real error: machine B's git push -u origin HEAD rejected non-fast-forward — CI's git left the branch BEHIND the remote after the convergence rebase. Fix (two parts, both shipped): (1) rebaseOntoUpstream prepends syncIdentityArgs to rebase/--continue/--skip (runners have no git identity; commits already had the fallback); (2) syncSetup's push self-heals: on non-fast-forward/behind/fetch-first stderr, re-fetch + branch -M + rebaseOntoUpstream + one retry push — a rebase fast-forwards a behind branch. If push still fails, errors embed a git log --oneline --all snapshot. Debugging convention: test asserts on sync results include JSON.stringify(result.errors) so CI names the git error instead of false!==true. CI green at 9b2f5a3." \ --paths "mcp/kernel.ts" "mcp/kernel.test.ts" --type bug_fix --tags ci sync git 2>/dev/null | /usr/bin/head -1: {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "Captured session learning: .agent_memory/packets/bug_fix-kage-sync-ci-fix-rebase-identity-fallback-self-healing-non-fast-forward-push-ret-53e8be25.json"} {"interrupted": false, "isImage": false, "noOutputExpected": false, "stderr": "", "stdout": "Captured session learning: .agent_memory/packets/bug_fix-kage-sync-ci-fix-rebase-identity-fallback-self-healing-non-fast-forward-push-ret-53e8be25.json"} - node mcp/dist/cli.js help --all 2>/dev/null | grep -cE "kage org" ; grep -n '
